figures.caption: Figure 1: Top (Phase I): A frozen ViT and slot attention process images. A trainable MLP router and projection head map raw aggregates to unit-norm embeddings, optimized via cross-entropy and a cross-correlation penalty. Bottom (Phase II): Gradient-free inference composes novel scenes by centering and matching slots via bidirectional Chamfer distance.
Table 18: αblend sensitivity on CGQA and COBJ. Default α=0.3 bold.

Table 6: Slot purity and NMI on COCO val2017 (K = 7, 5000 images) across different backbone and slot representations. ∆ vs GRU is computed relative to the GRU baseline of the corresponding backbone patch size. Attn src denotes the attention maps used for SCA readout.

Table 4: CFST benchmark, 10-way 10-shot (mean over 3 seeds: 42, 123, 7). ±ci = 95% CI. †: frozen DINOv2 ViT-B/14. ‡: CoFiMA also fine-tunes DINOv2 (≈4 h/dataset). ⋆: episodically meta-trained. §: single-seed result (seed 42 only). Bold = best overall; Underline = best frozen-backbone.

Table 2: Effect of self-supervised backbone on COMPOSE on the CGQA benchmark (10-way 10-shot, mean over 3 seeds: 42, 123, 7). All other COMPOSE components fixed (same DINOSAUR slot attention, same MLP router architecture). ‡: Results taken from [Liao et al., 2026]; all methods in that block use an ImageNet-21K supervised ViT-B/16 backbone. Bold = best noc; Underline = best Ha.

Table 11: Comparison with ViT-based CIL methods on standard benchmarks (single seed 42). AT = Pr[ŷ = y | (x, y) ∼ Dte 0:T ]: accuracy on the cumulative test set after the final session T (↑). AA = 1 T+1 ∑T t=0 At: average accuracy across all sessions (↑). FF: drop in base-class accuracy from session 0 to session T (↓). CIFAR-100†: 10 sessions × 10-way (100 total classes; session 1 is the base session on the first 10 classes, sessions 2–10 are incremental; all sessions update each method’s trainable components), images resized to 224 × 224. ImageNet-R/A: 10 sessions × 20-way (200 total classes; same convention). All methods use DINOv2 ViT-B/14 as backbone. Bold = best per column; Underline = lowest FF per column.
Table 9: Ablation results, 10-way 10-shot. Single seed = 42. ∆ relative to COMPOSE.
Table 3: Ablation results, 10-way 10-shot. Single seed = 42. ∆ relative to COMPOSE (CGQA/COBJ). Full per-split results in Appendix C.

Table 14: Gradient alignment Skk′ and accuracy for soft Chamfer variants (β sweep) vs. COMPOSE and COMPOSE-CT (hard), on CGQA. Skk′ ↑ means more uniform slot gradients (holistic); Skk′ ↓ means more slot-specific gradients. Accuracy is from a 3-session mini-benchmark (shorter than main Table 2).
